findings.summary: infrastructure/core pulls from infrastructure/project, inverting the dependency direction
evidence: infrastructure/core/cli.py line 35: `from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects` — a hard runtime import from a sibling domain into core

infrastructure/core/multi_project.py line 21: `from infrastructure.project.discovery import ProjectInfo` guarded by TYPE_CHECKING, partially mitigated but the type signature still binds core to project at the interface level
findings.suggestion: Define a minimal protocol or abstract type (e.g., a `ProjectInfo`-compatible dataclass) inside `infrastructure/core/` and have `infrastructure/project/discovery.py` conform to it. `infrastructure/core/cli.py` should accept the protocol, not import the concrete type. This keeps `core` dependency-free of `project`.

findings.summary: scripts/__init__.py executes a module at import time via importlib, blurring the script/library boundary
evidence: scripts/__init__.py lines 118-123: `_spec.loader.exec_module(_llm_review)` and `sys.modules[_module_name] = _llm_review` run unconditionally at import time, loading and executing 06_llm_review.py whenever the scripts package is imported
This means any test or tool that does `import scripts` triggers a full script load, conflating package metadata with runtime side effects
findings.suggestion: Move the dynamic load into a lazy function (e.g., `def get_llm_review_module()`) called only when needed, or refactor the shared symbols from 06_llm_review.py into an infrastructure module that can be imported directly without executing the script entry point.

findings.summary: infrastructure/validation/output_validator.py contains an inline import from infrastructure.project at the call site rather than the module boundary
evidence: infrastructure/validation/output_validator.py line 202: `from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects` inside a function body — a deferred cross-package import used to avoid a circular import rather than resolving the root coupling
findings.suggestion: Pass `project_names: set[str]` as a parameter to the validation function instead of having it discover projects internally. This removes the cross-package dependency and makes the function easier to test.

findings.summary: Several scripts in scripts/ omit get_logger/log_header/log_success that 15 of 18 sibling scripts use, creating style islands
evidence: Packet conventions data: scripts/ shared_patterns shows get_logger (15/18), log_header (13/18), log_success (11/18)
scripts/generate_test_summary.py has no get_logger, no log_header, no log_success — it delegates entirely to infrastructure.reporting without any local logging setup
scripts/execute_pipeline.py missing log_header and log_success despite being the primary pipeline entry point
scripts/__init__.py missing get_logger, log_header, log_success — instead it re-exports symbols from infrastructure and dynamically loads scripts
findings.suggestion: Apply the same logger initialization (`logger = get_logger(__name__)`) and use `log_header`/`log_success` consistently in all scripts/ entry points. For `scripts/__init__.py` specifically, the issue is structural (it is not a thin orchestrator), so fixing the underlying dynamic load pattern (see cross_module_architecture finding) resolves this outlier as a side effect.

findings.summary: One camelCase identifier observed in tests/ directory which is otherwise exclusively snake_case
evidence: Packet conventions data: tests/ naming_by_directory shows snake_case: 267, camelCase: 1 — a single camelCase identifier among 268 total naming tokens, indicating an isolated convention slip
findings.suggestion: Locate the camelCase identifier in tests/ (likely a test function or variable name) and rename it to snake_case to match the rest of the test suite.

findings.summary: scripts/__init__.py re-exports 22 symbols from infrastructure modules, obscuring the package's internal structure and purpose
evidence: scripts/__init__.py lines 92-182: re-exports get_max_input_length, check_ollama_availability, extract_manuscript_text, generate_executive_summary, generate_quality_review, generate_methodology_review, generate_improvement_suggestions, validate_review_quality, save_review_outputs, generate_review_summary, log_stage, is_off_topic, detect_conversational_phrases, check_format_compliance, ReviewMetrics, ManuscriptMetrics, SessionMetrics, estimate_tokens, LLMClient, LLMConfig, is_ollama_running, select_best_model — 22 symbols
The dimension description flags 'Large __init__.py re-export surfaces that obscure internal module structure' as a concrete convention_outlier pattern
findings.suggestion: Remove the re-export surface from scripts/__init__.py. Tests that need these symbols should import directly from the infrastructure modules where the symbols live (e.g., `from infrastructure.llm.review.generator import generate_executive_summary`). The scripts package should not act as a facade for infrastructure.

findings.summary: Multiple bare `except Exception: pass` blocks in infrastructure/validation/ silently discard errors with no logging or propagation
evidence: infrastructure/validation/repo_scanner.py lines 160-161: `except Exception: pass` inside `_scan_documentation()` — markdown parse failures are silently dropped
infrastructure/validation/repo_scanner.py lines 220-221, 247-248, 310, 366, 432: five additional bare `except Exception: pass` blocks across different methods
infrastructure/validation/doc_completeness.py lines 72-73: `except Exception: pass` when checking script docstrings
infrastructure/validation/doc_accuracy.py lines 238, 276, 294: three bare swallows with no logging
findings.suggestion: Replace bare `except Exception: pass` with `except Exception: logger.debug("...", exc_info=True)` at minimum. For parse operations, return an empty result with a logged warning rather than silently continuing. This matches the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., integrity.py line 77-78 logs before returning False).

findings.summary: I/O boundary functions inconsistently mix returns_null vs raises across sibling modules in the same validation layer
evidence: infrastructure/core/config_loader.py lines 133-161: `load_config()` returns None on all failure paths (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, YAMLError, unexpected Exception) — callers must null-check
infrastructure/validation/pdf_validator.py line 21: imports PDFValidationError; pdf_validator raises on failure
infrastructure/validation/integrity.py line 77: returns False (not raises) on per-file exception, but line 589 and 812 raise on aggregate failure — inconsistent strategy within the same module
Packet error data: validation/ strategy shows try_catch: 69, throws: 17, returns_null: 15 with result_type: 2 — four different strategies active in one module group
findings.suggestion: Establish a per-layer convention: validation functions that aggregate results (check N files) should return a structured result object; those at single-item boundaries should raise typed exceptions (using the existing TemplateError hierarchy). Document the convention in the validation/__init__.py docstring and audit the 15 returns_null cases against the chosen rule.

findings.summary: LLM review generator silently falls back to a simpler query on any exception, hiding streaming errors
evidence: infrastructure/llm/review/generator.py lines 783-784: `except Exception: response = client.query(prompt, options=options)` — any error in the streaming path (network, timeout, decode) triggers a silent fallback to a blocking query with no log entry at any level
findings.suggestion: Log the streaming exception before falling back: `except Exception as e: logger.warning("Streaming generation failed, falling back to blocking query: %s", e)`. This preserves the fallback behavior while making the error visible in pipeline logs for debugging.
